title: Modern Control Engineering
annotation: The standard undergraduate reference. Dense but comprehensive — transfer functions, root locus, frequency response, and state-space.

title: Feedback Control of Dynamic Systems
annotation: Strong on physical intuition and worked examples. Good for frequency-domain methods and digital control.

title: Modern Control Systems
annotation: Broad coverage of classical and modern control. Useful for root locus, PID tuning, and quick reference.

title: Making Embedded Systems
annotation: Practical patterns for firmware architecture. Best entry point for embedded software design — state machines, driver layering, RTOS concepts.

title: The Definitive Guide to ARM Cortex-M3 and Cortex-M4 Processors
annotation: Authoritative reference for the Cortex-M architecture, NVIC, memory model, and fault handling. The ground truth for ARM-core specifics. Covers M3 and M4; newer M33 core (EFR32xG21+) requires the ARM Architecture Reference Manual.

title: Programming Embedded Systems
annotation: Solid coverage of bare-metal C patterns and peripheral driver design. Practical and hardware-focused.

title: The Art of Electronics
annotation: The definitive practical electronics reference. Chapters on op-amps, ADCs, noise, and mixed-signal design are essential reading for any embedded hardware work.

title: Electromagnetic Compatibility Engineering
annotation: The standard reference for PCB EMC — ground planes, return current paths, filtering, and layout for emissions and immunity.

title: Linear System Theory and Design
annotation: Mathematically rigorous state-space treatment. Key reference for ECE 557 — controllability, observability, state feedback, observers, and the separation principle.

title: Feedback Systems: An Introduction for Scientists and Engineers
annotation: Freely available online. Emphasizes physical intuition and modern perspectives on feedback. Good companion to classical texts.

title: Physiological Control Systems: Analysis, Simulation, and Estimation
annotation: Primary textbook for BME 331. Applies linear control theory to physiological systems — endocrine, cardiovascular, and neuromuscular control. Bridges engineering and biology.

title: A First Course on Adaptive Control and Reinforcement Learning
annotation: UofT course notes bridging classical adaptive control and modern RL. Covers LQR, adaptive control, policy gradients, and Q-learning within a unified control-theoretic framework.

title: Signals and Systems
annotation: The definitive signals and systems text. Covers CTFT, DTFT, Laplace, Z-transform, convolution, sampling, and modulation. Primary textbook for ECE 355.

title: Linear Systems and Signals
annotation: Alternative signals text used as an ECE 355 reference. Clear exposition and good problem sets.

title: Fundamentals of Electric Circuits
annotation: Primary circuits textbook for ECE 159. Covers KVL/KCL, phasors, Thevenin/Norton, op-amps, and AC steady-state analysis. Standard introductory circuits reference.
source: SchoolStuff:Courses/Year 1/Term 2/ECE 159/- Fundamentals of Electric Circuits (7th edition) (2021).pdf

title: Microelectronic Circuits
annotation: Primary textbook for ECE 354. Covers BJT and MOSFET amplifiers, differential pairs, frequency response, feedback, and op-amp design. The standard microelectronics reference.

title: Analog Integrated Circuit Design
annotation: Primary reference for ECE 430. Covers CMOS op-amp design, noise, frequency compensation, folded-cascode topologies, and fully differential amplifiers.

title: Probability & Statistics for Engineers and Scientists
annotation: Primary textbook for ECE 286. Covers probability distributions, joint distributions, CLT, point and interval estimation, hypothesis testing, and regression analysis.

title: Introduction to Quantum Mechanics
annotation: The standard undergraduate quantum mechanics text. Clear pedagogical style, covers wave functions, Schrodinger equation, infinite square well, harmonic oscillator, hydrogen atom, and perturbation theory.

title: Modern Physics for Scientists and Engineers
annotation: Textbook for PHY 294 Part 1. Accessible introduction to special relativity, quantum mechanics, and atomic/nuclear physics. Good bridge from classical to quantum.

title: An Introduction to Thermal Physics
annotation: Textbook for PHY 294 Part 2 (thermal physics). Intuitive coverage of thermodynamics, statistical mechanics, and entropy. Unusually clear for a physics textbook.

title: Principles of Neural Science
annotation: The authoritative neuroscience reference. Comprehensive coverage from ion channels and synaptic transmission to motor systems, cognition, and disorders.

title: Neuroscience: Exploring the Brain
annotation: Accessible undergraduate neuroscience text. Strong on cellular and systems-level neuroscience without requiring a graduate background.

title: Rhythms of the Brain
annotation: Explores neural oscillations and brain rhythms — theta, gamma, sharp-wave ripples. Essential background for anyone thinking about neural signals and brain dynamics.

title: Neural Engineering
annotation: Primary textbook for ECE 441. Covers neural recording, stimulation, signal processing, brain-computer interfaces, EEG/fMRI, and neuromodulation applications.

title: Reinforcement Learning: An Introduction
annotation: The foundational RL textbook. Covers MDPs, dynamic programming, Monte Carlo methods, TD-learning, Q-learning, policy gradient methods, and deep RL connections.

title: Nonlinear Dynamics and Chaos
annotation: Exceptionally readable introduction to nonlinear dynamics — bifurcations, phase portraits, limit cycles, and chaos. Builds intuition through physical examples before formalism.

title: On Food and Cooking: The Science and Lore of the Kitchen
annotation: The foundational reference for applied food science. Covers the chemistry of cooking — Maillard reactions, protein denaturation, emulsification, fermentation, and why techniques work.
